essay: Mary Shelley write Frankenstein in 1847, and Jane Austen wrote Jane Eyre. Both novels shows how women are trapped by society.
expected_review_reason: Grammar can be auto-corrected, but authorship and publication-year corrections need teacher review.

essay: The monster is Victor Frankenstein, so the novel proves that people should never study knowledge.
expected_review_reason: The character confusion is KG-supported, while the broad interpretation changes student meaning.

essay: In comparison, the two books are same because both main characters want freedom.
expected_review_reason: The sentence needs academic phrasing, but a teacher should verify the intended contrast.

essay: Frankenstein is only about science and Jane Eyre is only about love, so they are not useful for studying society.
expected_review_reason: Absolute interpretive claims should be routed to review rather than blindly rewritten.

essay: Both novels shows the pressure of society, but they describe freedom in different ways.
expected_review_reason: A local subject-verb agreement correction can be safely auto-accepted.

essay: Charlotte Bronte wrote Pride and Prejudice, and Jane Austen wrote Jane Eyre. This shows the novels belong to the same author.
expected_review_reason: Wrong authorship claims require KG-supported review.

essay: Hamlet is a comedy because the ending makes the audience happy, and Macbeth is a romance about marriage.
expected_review_reason: Genre claims about Shakespeare plays require evidence and teacher review.

essay: In Wuthering Heights, Jane Eyre marries Heathcliff, so both novels describe the same romantic relationship.
expected_review_reason: Cross-work character confusion should be flagged as KG-supported review.

essay: The Picture of Dorian Gray was written by Charles Dickens, and it proves Victorian society had no concern with art.
expected_review_reason: Authorship correction and broad interpretation should not be auto-applied.

essay: Dracula is written by Mary Shelley, and the novel is mainly about industrial education.
expected_review_reason: Authorship correction is factual, while theme framing needs teacher review.

essay: Moby-Dick are a simple story about a friendly whale, and the narrator never thinks about obsession.
expected_review_reason: Agreement error is low-risk; interpretation is high-risk.

essay: The Scarlet Letter was published in 1925, so it is a modernist novel like The Great Gatsby.
expected_review_reason: Publication-year and movement claims need KG grounding.

essay: The Great Gatsby was wrote by Virginia Woolf, and Nick is not important to the story.
expected_review_reason: Verb form can be corrected, but authorship and character importance need review.

essay: To the Lighthouse and Mrs Dalloway is both about one day, so they have exactly the same structure.
expected_review_reason: Grammar can be fixed; structural equivalence is interpretive.

essay: Heart of Darkness is a cheerful adventure novel, so Conrad supports colonialism without any criticism.
expected_review_reason: Potentially oversimplified interpretation should be routed to teacher review.

essay: The Turn of the Screw is by Jane Austen, and the ghosts are definitely real in every reading.
expected_review_reason: Authorship is factual; interpretive certainty is high-risk.

essay: The Awakening shows that Edna never wants independence, and all readers should agree with this.
expected_review_reason: Interpretive claim changes meaning and should be reviewed.

essay: The Yellow Wallpaper are a poem about happy marriage.
expected_review_reason: Agreement can be fixed, but genre and theme claims need evidence.

essay: Middlemarch was written by Emily Bronte, and Dorothea is a minor character.
expected_review_reason: Wrong author and character claim require KG-supported review.

essay: Tess of the d'Urbervilles is a Shakespeare tragedy, and Tess chooses her fate freely.
expected_review_reason: Genre and interpretation should be checked.

essay: The Mayor of Casterbridge was wrote by Oscar Wilde, and it only criticizes poor people.
expected_review_reason: Verb form can be fixed; author and thematic claim require review.

essay: The Importance of Being Earnest is a serious tragedy with no humor, because Wilde wanted only moral instruction.
expected_review_reason: Genre and authorial-intent claims need teacher review.

essay: A Doll's House is a British novel by Charles Dickens, so Nora is similar to Jane Eyre because both are written by men.
expected_review_reason: Form, author and comparative claim need KG evidence and review.

essay: The Odyssey was wrote by Shakespeare, and Odysseus is a modern Victorian hero.
expected_review_reason: Grammar can be corrected; authorship and historical framing need review.

essay: Oliver Twist and Great Expectations shows Dickens's interest in childhood, but the essay need a clearer contrast.
expected_review_reason: Low-risk grammar and academic-style suggestions are expected.

essay: A Tale of Two Cities is about the French Revolution, but it are also the same as Pride and Prejudice.
expected_review_reason: Grammar can be auto-accepted; comparison needs teacher review.

essay: Othello is a novel about jealousy, and Desdemona writes the whole story in first person.
expected_review_reason: Genre and narrator claims require correction.

essay: King Lear is the youngest daughter who divides the kingdom, so Cordelia is the villain.
expected_review_reason: Character-role confusion and interpretation need review.

essay: Macbeth shows ambition, but Macbeth and Lady Macbeth is exactly identical in motivation.
expected_review_reason: Grammar can be fixed; interpretation needs review.

essay: Pride and Prejudice are about marriage and class, but my paragraph does not explain why Elizabeth's judgment changes.
expected_review_reason: Grammar can be auto-accepted while thesis development remains teacher-facing.
